title: cymopterus humilis
intro: Cymopterus humilis, also known as low desert springs parsley, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family native to the southwestern United States. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including alkaloids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cymopterus humilis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
